perturbation.original_text: 6. COMPENSATION. In consideration of the services provided, SRP shall be entitled to a base fee of Eighty Thousand Dollars and No/100's (580,000.00) payable as follows: (i) $40,000.00 payable upon execution of this contract; and (ii) the balance of $40,000.00 payable in eight equal monthly installments of $5,000 each, due the first day of March, April, May, June, July, August, September and October, 1998.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. COMPENSATION. In consideration of the services provided, SRP shall be entitled to a base fee of Eighty Thousand Dollars and No/100's (580,000.00) payable as follows: (i) $40,000.00 payable upon execution of this contract; and (ii) the balance of $40,000.00 payable in eight equal monthly installments of $5,000 each, due the first day of March, April, May, June, July, August, September and November, 1998.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the payment schedule in Section 6. Originally, the final payment was due in October, 1998. The modification extends the payments through November, 1998, which is in conflict with the Term defined in Section 2. TERM. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the date hereof and, unless terminated as provided herein, shall continue through November 30, 1998. Payments typically should be due within the contract term, with that late payment, create the uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. TERM. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the date hereof and, unless terminated as provided herein, shall continue through November 30, 1998.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 6 extends the payment schedule through November 1998, while Section 2 specifies that the agreement's term ends on November 30, 1998. While the change does not cause the agreement to extend further, the contradiction stems from payments that typically should be due within the contract term. The extended payment creates uncertainty in enforcement since payments now occur at the very end of the term, which could lead to disputes.

perturbation.original_text: 10. SPECIAL RIGHT OF TERMINATION. Either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement, subject to the following terms and conditions: (a) Either party ("the Terminating Party") may terminate this Agreement upon prior written notice to the other party (the "Defaulting Party") upon the occurrence of any of the following conditions: (i) In the event that such party materially breaches this Agreement or materially defaults in the performance of any obligation hereunder and fails to cure said breach within 30 days of written notice to the Defaulting Party by the Terminating Party; (ii) Immediately in the event that the Defaulting Party (including, for SRP, the Driver) commits any crime involving moral turpitude or otherwise commits any act or is involved in any situation bringing itself, the Terminating Party or the products of the Terminating Party into public hatred or contempt, or engages in conduct that shocks or insults the community or brings the Terminating Party, or its products into public disrespect, scandal or ridicule; or (iii) In the event the LMSC series at NMS is canceled. (b) In the event Logan's terminates this Agreement pursuant to this Article 10, Logan's shall be entitled to a pro rata refund of amounts prepaid for the current calendar year, with no further obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. SPECIAL RIGHT OF TERMINATION. Either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement, subject to the following terms and conditions: (a) Either party ("the Terminating Party") may terminate this Agreement upon prior written notice to the other party (the "Defaulting Party") upon the occurrence of any of the following conditions: (i) In the event that such party materially breaches this Agreement or materially defaults in the performance of any obligation hereunder and fails to cure said breach within 30 days of written notice to the Defaulting Party by the Terminating Party; (ii) Immediately in the event that the Defaulting Party (including, for SRP, the Driver) commits any crime involving moral turpitude or otherwise commits any act or is involved in any situation bringing itself, the Terminating Party or the products of the Terminating Party into public hatred or contempt, or engages in conduct that shocks or insults the community or brings the Terminating Party, or its products into public disrespect, scandal or ridicule; or (iii) In the event the LMSC series at NMS is canceled. (b) In the event Logan's terminates this Agreement pursuant to this Article 10, Logan's shall be entitled to a full refund of amounts prepaid for the current calendar year, with no further obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 10(b) creates an in-text contradiction. Originally, if Logan's terminates the agreement under Article 10, they receive a 'pro rata refund' of prepaid amounts. The modification changes this to a 'full refund'. This contradicts the established pro rata refund mentioned initially, creating uncertainty about the actual refund amount Logan's is entitled to upon termination, making the 'pro rata' concept a contradiction, as it is no longer valid, as a full refund would be given instead.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. SPECIAL RIGHT OF TERMINATION. Either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement, subject to the following terms and conditions: (a) Either party ("the Terminating Party") may terminate this Agreement upon prior written notice to the other party (the "Defaulting Party") upon the occurrence of any of the following conditions: (i) In the event that such party materially breaches this Agreement or materially defaults in the performance of any obligation hereunder and fails to cure said breach within 30 days of written notice to the Defaulting Party by the Terminating Party; (ii) Immediately in the event that the Defaulting Party (including, for SRP, the Driver) commits any crime involving moral turpitude or otherwise commits any act or is involved in any situation bringing itself, the Terminating Party or the products of the Terminating Party into public hatred or contempt, or engages in conduct that shocks or insults the community or brings the Terminating Party, or its products into public disrespect, scandal or ridicule; or (iii) In the event the LMSC series at NMS is canceled. (b) In the event Logan's terminates this Agreement pursuant to this Article 10, Logan's shall be entitled to a full refund of amounts prepaid for the current calendar year, with no further obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 10(b) to state that Logan's will receive a 'full refund' upon termination under Article 10, while the original text stated Logan's was entitled to a 'pro rata refund'. This direct replacement of 'pro rata' with 'full' creates an in-text contradiction because the terms are mutually exclusive methods of calculating a refund, thus creating an inconsistency within the document. This fits the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 13. INSURANCE. SRP shall provide at its expense, and maintain throughout the term of this Agreement, comprehensive general liability insurance in the amount of Five Million Dollars and No/100's ($5,000,000.00) per occurrence with respect to any liability relating to the activities of SRP in the performance of this Agreement SRP shall supply Logan's with a copy of the Certificate of Insurance naming Logan's as an additional insured. Such policies shall provide for at least fifteen (15) days' written notice to Logan's of the cancellation or substantial modification thereof. Such insurance will be supplemental coverage in excess of the Five Million Dollars ($5,000,000.00) primary coverage provided to SRP and Logan's by the comprehensive liability policy of NMS.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. INSURANCE. SRP shall provide at its expense, and maintain throughout the term of this Agreement, comprehensive general liability insurance in the amount of Five Million Dollars and No/100's ($5,000,000.00) per occurrence with respect to any liability relating to the activities of SRP in the performance of this Agreement SRP shall supply Logan's with a copy of the Certificate of Insurance naming Logan's as an additional insured. Such policies shall provide for at least fifteen (15) days' written notice to Logan's of the cancellation or substantial modification thereof. Such insurance will be primary coverage alongside the Five Million Dollars ($5,000,000.00) primary coverage provided to SRP and Logan's by the comprehensive liability policy of NMS.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction in Section 13 regarding the nature of the insurance coverage. Initially, SRP's insurance is defined as 'supplemental coverage in excess of' the NMS policy. The modification alters this to 'primary coverage alongside' the NMS policy. This contradicts the original definition, creating ambiguity and potential disputes about which policy is primarily responsible in case of a claim. The phrase 'supplemental coverage in excess of' indicates that SRP's policy would only be used after the NMS policy is exhausted. However, 'primary coverage alongside' implies both policies share primary responsibility, which directly contradicts the initial understanding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13. INSURANCE. SRP shall provide at its expense, and maintain throughout the term of this Agreement, comprehensive general liability insurance in the amount of Five Million Dollars and No/100's ($5,000,000.00) per occurrence with respect to any liability relating to the activities of SRP in the performance of this Agreement SRP shall supply Logan's with a copy of the Certificate of Insurance naming Logan's as an additional insured. Such policies shall provide for at least fifteen (15) days' written notice to Logan's of the cancellation or substantial modification thereof. Such insurance will be primary coverage alongside the Five Million Dollars ($5,000,000.00) primary coverage provided to SRP and Logan's by the comprehensive liability policy of NMS.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the insurance coverage from 'supplemental coverage in excess of' to 'primary coverage alongside'. This creates a direct contradiction within Section 13 itself (since the Contradicted Text is also Section 13). The original understanding was that SRP's policy would only be used after NMS's policy was exhausted, which is not compatible with the new statement implying both policies share primary responsibility simultaneously, therefore directly contradicting the original statement.
